Explore the DDx codebase to understand the bead tracker implementation. I need to implement "auto-close epics when all children reach terminal state" plus a `ddx bead reap` command.

Please find and read:
1. The main bead package: look in `cli/internal/bead/` for how beads are stored, how close works, what "epic" means, parent/child relationships
2. The close command: `cli/cmd/bead_close.go` or similar
3. The bead list/query command to understand how to find epics with all-terminal children
4. Any existing tests in the bead package

Report back:
- Full path and content of the bead model/store files (types, Store struct, methods)
- How `ddx bead close` is implemented
- What fields a bead has (status values, parent, children, labels)
- Where `docs/beads.md` is located
- Any existing reap/sweep commands

Be thorough - read full file contents for the core bead package files.

1	package bead
2	
3	import (
4		"fmt"
5		"strings"
6		"time"
7	)
8	
9	// Bead represents a portable work item with metadata.
10	// The schema matches bd/br JSONL format for interchange compatibility.
11	// Unknown fields from external sources are preserved in Extra.
12	type Bead struct {
13		ID            string    `json:"id"`
14		Title         string    `json:"title"`
15		Status        string    `json:"status"`
16		Priority      int       `json:"priority"`
17		IssueType     string    `json:"issue_type"`
18		SchemaVersion int       `json:"schema_version"`
19		Owner         string    `json:"owner,omitempty"`
20		CreatedAt     time.Time `json:"created_at"`
21		CreatedBy     string    `json:"created_by,omitempty"`
22		UpdatedAt     time.Time `json:"updated_at"`
23	
24		// Optional fields (bd-compatible)
25		Labels      []string `json:"labels,omitempty"`
26		Parent      string   `json:"parent,omitempty"`
27		Description string   `json:"description,omitempty"`
28		Acceptance  string   `json:"acceptance,omitempty"`
29		Notes       string   `json:"notes,omitempty"`
30	
31		// Dependencies use bd-compatible format
32		Dependencies []Dependency `json:"dependencies,omitempty"`
33	
34		// Extra holds unknown fields for round-trip preservation.
35		// Workflow-specific fields (e.g. HELIX spec-id, execution-eligible)
36		// are stored here and written back on save.
37		Extra map[string]any `json:"-"`
38	}
39	
40	// Dependency represents a link between two beads (bd-compatible format).
41	type Dependency struct {
42		IssueID     string `json:"issue_id"`
43		DependsOnID string `json:"depends_on_id"`
44		Type        string `json:"type"` // "blocks", "related", etc.
45		CreatedAt   string `json:"created_at,omitempty"`
46		CreatedBy   string `json:"created_by,omitempty"`
47		Metadata    string `json:"metadata,omitempty"`
48	}
49	
50	// BeadEvent records append-only execution evidence.
51	type BeadEvent struct {
52		Kind      string    `json:"kind"`
53		Summary   string    `json:"summary,omitempty"`
54		Body      string    `json:"body,omitempty"`
55		Actor     string    `json:"actor,omitempty"`
56		CreatedAt time.Time `json:"created_at"`
57		Source    string    `json:"source,omitempty"`
58	}
59	
60	// Status constants
61	const (
62		StatusOpen       = "open"
63		StatusInProgress = "in_progress"
64		StatusClosed     = "closed"
65		// StatusBlocked marks an explicit external blocker that can be rechecked.
66		// Dependency waits are derived from dependency status and do not persist as
67		// blocked.
68		StatusBlocked = "blocked"
69		// StatusProposed marks work awaiting operator approval or clarification.
70		// It is outside worker dispatch until transitioned to open or cancelled.
71		StatusProposed = "proposed"
72		// StatusCancelled marks work that will not run and does not satisfy
73		// dependents.
74		StatusCancelled = "cancelled"
75	)
76	
77	// CanonicalStatuses is the single source of truth for the persisted bead
78	// status enumeration. It mirrors the bd/br canonical set documented in
79	// TD-031 §2 and the JSON Schema enum at
80	// cli/internal/bead/schema/bead-record.schema.json. The CI guard tests in
81	// sync_test.go assert that schema, TD doc, and Go source agree with this
82	// list; adding or removing a value here without updating the schema and TD
83	// will fail CI.
84	var CanonicalStatuses = []string{
85		StatusOpen,
86		StatusInProgress,
87		StatusClosed,
88		StatusBlocked,
89		StatusProposed,
90		StatusCancelled,
91	}
92	
93	// IsCanonicalStatus reports whether s is one of the persisted bead statuses
94	// in CanonicalStatuses.
95	func IsCanonicalStatus(s string) bool {
96		for _, c := range CanonicalStatuses {
97			if c == s {
98				return true
99			}
100		}
101		return false
102	}
103	
104	// IssueType constants
105	const (
106		// IssueTypeOperatorPrompt is the bead type used for operator-submitted
107		// prompts that the work runs as instructions.
108		IssueTypeOperatorPrompt = "operator-prompt"
109		// IssueTypeReviewFinding is a bead kind for findings produced by review engines.
110		// Parent must reference the bead under review.
111		IssueTypeReviewFinding = "review-finding"
112		// IssueTypeAlignmentReview is a bead kind for alignment review work.
113		// Parent must reference the bead under alignment.
114		IssueTypeAlignmentReview = "alignment-review"
115	)
116	
117	// Default labels and other defaults for operator-prompt beads.
118	const (
119		OperatorPromptLabelKind   = "kind:operator-prompt"
120		OperatorPromptLabelSource = "source:web-ui"
121		// OperatorPromptDefaultAcceptance is the auto-AC stub used when an
122		// operator submits a prompt without explicit acceptance criteria. The
123		// structural AC verifier is skipped for operator-prompt beads, so this
124		// stub stands as a human-readable contract only.
125		OperatorPromptDefaultAcceptance = "Agent must produce a diff or no_changes rationale; the prompt body is the contract."
126	)
127	
128	// OperatorPromptMutationGuard enforces the no-self-mutation rule from
129	// Story 15: an operator-prompt bead's execution may not create, edit, or
130	// close another operator-prompt bead. The guard returns nil when the
131	// mutation is allowed and a non-nil error when it must be rejected.
132	//
133	// actorIssueType is the issue_type of the bead currently being executed
134	// (empty when no operator-prompt context is active). targetIssueType is
135	// the issue_type of the bead about to be mutated.
136	//
137	// Allow/deny matrix:
138	//
139	//	actor=""               , target=*                → allow
140	//	actor="task"           , target=*                → allow
141	//	actor="operator-prompt", target!="operator-prompt" → allow
142	//	actor="operator-prompt", target=="operator-prompt" → deny
143	func OperatorPromptMutationGuard(actorIssueType, targetIssueType string) error {
144		if actorIssueType != IssueTypeOperatorPrompt {
145			return nil
146		}
147		if targetIssueType != IssueTypeOperatorPrompt {
148			return nil
149		}
150		return fmt.Errorf("bead: operator-prompt bead may not mutate another operator-prompt bead")
151	}
152	
153	// NewOperatorPromptBead constructs a fresh operator-prompt bead from a raw
154	// prompt string, applying the Story 15 template defaults: title is the first
155	// non-empty line of the prompt, full prompt body is preserved verbatim in the
156	// description, default labels are kind:operator-prompt + source:web-ui, the
157	// status starts in `proposed` (approval flow), priority defaults to the
158	// caller-supplied priority (clamped to MinPriority..MaxPriority), and the
159	// acceptance field carries the auto-AC stub.
160	//
161	// The returned bead is not persisted; callers feed it to Store.Create which
162	// will assign the ID and CreatedAt/UpdatedAt timestamps.
163	func NewOperatorPromptBead(prompt string, defaultTier int) *Bead {
164		body := strings.TrimSpace(prompt)
165		title := body
166		if i := strings.IndexByte(body, '\n'); i >= 0 {
167			title = strings.TrimSpace(body[:i])
168		}
169		if title == "" {
170			title = "(empty operator prompt)"
171		}
172		priority := defaultTier
173		if priority < MinPriority {
174			priority = MinPriority
175		}
176		if priority > MaxPriority {
177			priority = MaxPriority
178		}
179		return &Bead{
180			Title:       title,
181			IssueType:   IssueTypeOperatorPrompt,
182			Status:      StatusProposed,
183			Priority:    priority,
184			Labels:      []string{OperatorPromptLabelKind, OperatorPromptLabelSource},
185			Description: body,
186			Acceptance:  OperatorPromptDefaultAcceptance,
187		}
188	}
189	
190	// Default values
191	const (
192		DefaultType          = "task"
193		DefaultStatus        = StatusOpen
194		DefaultPriority      = 2
195		DefaultPrefix        = "bx" // used only when repo name detection fails
196		CurrentSchemaVersion = 1
197		MinPriority          = 0
198		MaxPriority          = 4
199	)
200	
201	// StatusCounts holds aggregate counts for a bead store.
202	type StatusCounts struct {
203		Open              int `json:"open"`
204		InProgress        int `json:"in_progress"`
205		Closed            int `json:"closed"`
206		Blocked           int `json:"blocked"`
207		Proposed          int `json:"proposed"`
208		Cancelled         int `json:"cancelled"`
209		Ready             int `json:"ready"`
210		NeedsHuman        int `json:"needs_human"`
211		WorkerReady       int `json:"worker_ready"`
212		DependencyWaiting int `json:"dependency_waiting"`
213		ExternalBlocked   int `json:"external_blocked"`
214		OperatorAttention int `json:"operator_attention"`
215		Total             int `json:"total"`
216	}
217	
218	// Blocker kinds surfaced through BlockedAll. These strings are part of the
219	// external DDx contract (HELIX reads them to decide how to handle a blocker).
220	const (
221		BlockerKindDependency         = "dependency"
222		BlockerKindBlockedStatus      = "blocked-status"
223		BlockerKindRetryCooldown      = "retry-cooldown"
224		BlockerKindNeedsInvestigation = "needs-investigation"
225		BlockerKindOperatorAttention  = "operator-attention"
226		BlockerKindNotEligible        = "not-execution-eligible"
227		BlockerKindSuperseded         = "superseded"
228		BlockerKindEpicOnly           = "epic-only"
229	)
230	
231	// Blocker describes why an open bead is currently not runnable. Either
232	// unclosed dependencies exist, or an work cooldown has parked the
233	// bead until NextEligibleAt.
234	type Blocker struct {
235		Kind           string   `json:"kind"`
236		NextEligibleAt string   `json:"next_eligible_at,omitempty"`
237		UnclosedDepIDs []string `json:"unclosed_dep_ids,omitempty"`
238		LastStatus     string   `json:"last_status,omitempty"`
239		LastDetail     string   `json:"last_detail,omitempty"`
240		Reason         string   `json:"reason,omitempty"`
241	}
242	
243	// BlockedBead pairs a bead with its blocker classification.
244	type BlockedBead struct {
245		Bead
246		Blocker Blocker `json:"blocker"`
247	}
248	
249	// DepIDs returns a flat list of dependency IDs for this bead.
250	func (b *Bead) DepIDs() []string {
251		var ids []string
252		for _, d := range b.Dependencies {
253			ids = append(ids, d.DependsOnID)
254		}
255		return ids
256	}
257	
258	// HasDep returns true if the bead depends on the given ID.
259	func (b *Bead) HasDep(id string) bool {
260		for _, d := range b.Dependencies {
261			if d.DependsOnID == id {
262				return true
263			}
264		}
265		return false
266	}
267	
268	// AddDep adds a dependency if it doesn't already exist.
269	func (b *Bead) AddDep(depID, depType string) {
270		for _, d := range b.Dependencies {
271			if d.DependsOnID == depID {
272				return // already exists
273			}
274		}
275		b.Dependencies = append(b.Dependencies, Dependency{
276			IssueID:     b.ID,
277			DependsOnID: depID,
278			Type:        depType,
279			CreatedAt:   time.Now().UTC().Format(time.RFC3339),
280		})
281	}
282	
283	// RemoveDep removes a dependency by target ID.
284	func (b *Bead) RemoveDep(depID string) {
285		var filtered []Dependency
286		for _, d := range b.Dependencies {
287			if d.DependsOnID != depID {
288				filtered = append(filtered, d)
289			}
290		}
291		b.Dependencies = filtered
292	}

184			return commitSHA, nil

190			return "", fmt.Errorf("git rev-parse %s: %w", commitSHA, err)
191		}
192		return strings.TrimSpace(string(out)), nil
193	}

97	func ValidateLifecycleTransition(from, to string, opts LifecycleTransitionOptions) error {
98		if err := validateLifecycleStatus(from); err != nil {

100		}
101		if err := validateLifecycleStatus(to); err != nil {
102			return err
103		}
104		if from == to {
105			return nil
106		}
107		if to == StatusClosed && opts.ManualClose {

110				return nil
111			}

113		if from == StatusClosed && to == StatusOpen && opts.ManualReopen {
114			return nil
115		}
116		if from == StatusClosed || from == StatusCancelled {
117			return lifecycleTransitionError(LifecycleIssueTransitionFromTerminal, from, to)
118		}
119		if to == StatusProposed && !opts.OperatorRequired {
120			return lifecycleTransitionError(LifecycleIssueTransitionRequiresOperator, from, to)
121		}
122		if to == StatusBlocked && strings.TrimSpace(opts.ExternalBlockerReason) == "" {
123			return lifecycleTransitionError(LifecycleIssueTransitionRequiresExternal, from, to)
124		}
125		if lifecycleTransitionAllowed(from, to) {
126			return nil
127		}
128		return lifecycleTransitionError(LifecycleIssueTransitionUnsupported, from, to)
129	}

```go
func (s *Store) walkUpClosureCandidate(parentID string, allBeads []Bead, visited map[string]bool) {
    parent := findBeadByID(allBeads, parentID)
    if parent == nil {
        return
    }
    
    // Count children
    openChildCount := 0
    totalChildCount := 0
    for _, b := range allBeads {
        if b.Parent == parentID {
            totalChildCount++
            if b.Status != StatusClosed {
                openChildCount++
            }
        }
    }
    
    // Closure criteria: execution-eligible==false AND no open children AND has children
    executionEligible, executionEligibleKnown := lifecycleExecutionEligible(*parent)
    if !executionEligibleKnown || executionEligible {
        return
    }
    if openChildCount > 0 || totalChildCount == 0 {
        return
    }
    
    // Auto-close the parent
    _ = s.SetLifecycleStatus(parentID, StatusClosed, ...)
}
```

**Key observation**: The current logic **only checks for `StatusClosed` children** (line 1632). To auto-close epics when ALL children are terminal (closed OR cancelled), you'd need to check if `b.Status == StatusClosed || b.Status == StatusCancelled`.

### 3. **Epic Detection Logic**
**File**: `/Users/erik/Projects/.ddx-exec-wt/.execute-bead-wt-ddx-c726d474-20260531T193558-d92561a5/cli/internal/bead/store.go` (lines 2622-2638)

```go
func isOrdinaryEpicContainer(b Bead, openChildCount, totalChildCount int) bool {
    issueType := strings.ToLower(strings.TrimSpace(b.IssueType))
    title := strings.ToLower(strings.TrimSpace(b.Title))
    explicitEpic := issueType == "epic" || strings.HasPrefix(title, "epic:")
    looseEpic := strings.HasPrefix(title, "epic ") && totalChildCount > 0
    if !explicitEpic && !looseEpic {
        return false
    }
    return totalChildCount > 0
}

func isEpicClosureCandidate(b Bead, openChildCount, totalChildCount int) bool {
    if totalChildCount == 0 {
        return false
    }
    return isOrdinaryEpicContainer(b, openChildCount, totalChildCount) && openChildCount == 0
}
```

**An epic is identified by**:
- `IssueType == "epic"` (explicit), OR
- Title starts with `"epic:"` (explicit), OR
- Title starts with `"epic "` AND has children (loose detection)

**Closure candidate**: An epic with all children closed AND has children.

14	// newBeadDoctorCommand wires `ddx bead doctor` / `ddx bead doctor --fix`.
15	//
16	// Scan mode (no flags): exits non-zero if any field on any bead exceeds the
17	// per-field cap (ddx-f8a11202), reporting the offending bead id, field, and
18	// size. Safe to run on any tree — no mutations.
19	//
20	// Fix mode (--fix): rewrites oversized fields in place. Before touching the
21	// tracker the command writes a timestamped backup under .ddx/backups/ so
22	// the original file is always recoverable. Overflow content persists as
23	// artifacts under .ddx/executions/<bead-id>/repair-<timestamp>/ and a
24	// repair event is appended to each rewritten bead. Idempotent — the second
25	// invocation exits 0 without writing because the scan is clean.
26	//
27	// Dangling-success mode (--dangling): detects in_progress beads whose last
28	// execution produced task_succeeded but CloseWithEvidence never ran. Safe
29	// to run on any tree — no mutations unless --fix is also passed.
30	// (ddx-2b2d114e)
31	func (f *CommandFactory) newBeadDoctorCommand() *cobra.Command {
32		cmd := &cobra.Command{
33			Use:   "doctor",
34			Short: "Detect (and optionally repair) beads.jsonl rows with oversized fields",
35			Long: `Scan the bead tracker for fields that exceed the per-field size cap.
36	
37	Bead fields (description, acceptance, notes, events[].body, events[].summary)
38	are capped at 65,535 bytes so DDx-authored beads round-trip cleanly through
39	bd import (upstream's Dolt TEXT column limit). Fields over the cap usually
40	come from a writer bug that landed before the cap was enforced — for
41	example a reviewer stream dumped verbatim into an event body.
42	
43	Without --fix this command only reports offending rows and exits non-zero.
44	With --fix it:
45	
46	  1. Writes a timestamped backup to .ddx/backups/ before any mutation.
47	  2. Truncates each oversized field to the cap using head+tail+marker.
48	  3. Writes the full original payload to
49	     .ddx/executions/<bead>/repair-<timestamp>/<field>.log so forensics
50	     remain possible.
51	  4. Appends a kind=repair event to every rewritten bead.
52	
53	Idempotent: once a tracker is clean, running --fix again is a no-op.
54	
55	With --dangling: scan for in_progress beads whose last execution produced
56	task_succeeded but whose bead-close step never ran (dangling-success).
57	For each finding, reports whether result_rev is reachable from HEAD:
58	  - reachable=true:  merge succeeded, close didn't. Safe to close manually
59	    with: ddx bead close <bead-id>
60	  - reachable=false: the merge itself was never applied (dangling commit).

# Bead Lifecycle

A **bead** is the atomic work item in DDx. This document covers lifecycle
statuses, transitions, and automatic housekeeping rules.

## Statuses

| Status        | Meaning                                           | Terminal? |
|---------------|---------------------------------------------------|-----------|
| `open`        | Ready or waiting for work                         | No        |
| `in_progress` | Claimed by an agent or operator                   | No        |
| `blocked`     | Waiting on an external blocker                    | No        |
| `proposed`    | Needs operator review before entering the queue   | No        |
| `closed`      | Work complete and verified                        | **Yes**   |
| `cancelled`   | Work abandoned; will not be revisited             | **Yes**   |

A bead in a terminal status cannot transition back to a non-terminal status
without an explicit `ddx bead reopen`.

## Epic auto-close

An **epic** is a bead whose `IssueType` is `"epic"` or whose title begins with
`"epic:"` or `"epic "` and that has at least one child bead.

When the **last non-terminal child** of an epic transitions to `closed` or
`cancelled`, DDx automatically closes the parent epic if the epic has
`execution_eligible: false` set (the standard flag for container beads). This
happens inline as part of `ddx bead close` via the walk-up closure pass
(`walkUpClosureCandidate`). Both `closed` and `cancelled` children count as
terminal for this purpose.

The auto-close appends a `dead_intermediate_close` event to the epic with
`closed_because: all_children_terminal`.

## `ddx bead reap`

For epics that were created without `execution_eligible: false` — or that
accumulated cancelled children before the auto-close logic was in place —
`ddx bead reap` provides a batch sweep:

```
ddx bead reap           # list epics eligible for closure
ddx bead reap --apply   # close them all in one pass
```

An epic qualifies when:

1. It has at least one child bead.
2. Every child is `closed` or `cancelled`.
3. The epic itself is `open` or `in_progress`.
4. It is detected as an epic container (IssueType or title prefix).

Epics with no children are left alone.
